description: Added E0003 missing-end EOF diagnostics anchored to opening construct spans, plus parser/CLI coverage for missing module/function/if end cases.
Added E0004 unexpected-arrow parser diagnostics with fix hints, plus parser/CLI coverage for bare arrow outside branch contexts.
Added E0005 stray block keyword parser diagnostics with fix hints, plus parser/CLI coverage for stray else/rescue cases.
Added E0006 missing-do parser diagnostics anchored to block-opening spans, plus parser/CLI coverage for representative missing-do cases.
Added E0007 missing-arrow clause diagnostics anchored to clause starts, plus parser/CLI coverage for representative case/rescue/fn missing-arrow failures.
Tried actionable E2001 operator mismatch diagnostics across arithmetic/comparison/unary-minus/integer-only operators, but regressed dynamic arithmetic typing semantics.
Added diagnostic-only E2001 bool/atom mismatch hints on existing typing failure paths, plus representative typing/CLI coverage.
Added actionable E3001/E3002 typing hints on existing failure paths, plus representative typing/CLI coverage.
Added diagnostic-only E2002 arity mismatch hints with source locations for user-defined, builtin, guard, and capture call failures, plus representative typing/CLI coverage.
Added diagnostic-only E1001 undefined-symbol suggestion plumbing for local/imported/module-qualified call typos, plus representative resolver/CLI coverage.
Added shared parser-side E0008 missing-map-entry separator diagnostics for map literals and patterns, plus representative parser/CLI coverage.
Added parser-side E0009 capture and anonymous-function diagnostics with repair hints, plus representative parser/CLI coverage.
Added diagnostic-only E2001 numeric operand hints on existing int-only mismatch paths, plus representative typing/CLI coverage.
Added parser-side E0010 missing-comma diagnostics for call arguments and function/protocol parameter lists, plus representative parser/CLI coverage.
Extended parser-side E0010 missing-comma diagnostics to with/for clause separators, plus representative parser/CLI coverage.
Extended parser-side E0002 unclosed-delimiter diagnostics to grouped expressions, calls/captures, index access, and function/protocol parameter lists, plus representative parser/CLI coverage.
Added parser-side E0010/E0002 bitstring separator and unclosed-delimiter diagnostics for bitstring literals and patterns, plus representative parser/CLI coverage.
Extended parser-side E0010/E0002 diagnostics to alias child lists, import filter lists, and structured raise keyword arguments, plus representative parser/CLI coverage.
Extended parser-side E0010/E0002 diagnostics to remaining tuple/list/keyword/map/struct literal and pattern delimiter/separator failures, plus representative parser/CLI coverage.

code_quality.rationale: Shared ReplSession state and focused server tests make the new remote REPL slice coherent and verifiable, though the transport is still a minimal newline-delimited JSON protocol rather than fuller nREPL semantics.

description: Extracted a shared ReplSession core and added tonic repl --listen with JSON/TCP eval, clear, and load-file operations plus focused server coverage.

code_quality.rationale: The logical-session layer cleanly extends the prior REPL server with reconnect, clone, and close behavior while keeping the implementation small and well-covered, though the protocol is still a minimal in-memory JSON/TCP substrate rather than fuller nREPL semantics.

description: Added server-wide logical REPL session ids with session-addressed eval/clear/load-file plus clone/close lifecycle ops and focused reconnect coverage.

code_quality.rationale: The describe op fits the existing REPL server cleanly, advertises capabilities in a compact schema, and is backed by focused unit and TCP integration coverage, though it is still a minimal custom protocol slice rather than fuller nREPL semantics.

description: Added a describe op that advertises supported remote REPL ops plus logical-session semantics, with focused unit and TCP integration coverage.

code_quality.rationale: The output-capture slice is a clean, focused extension of the existing REPL server: it centralizes host stdout/stderr routing in shared interop helpers, threads captured output into eval/load-file responses without widening scope, and adds focused unit plus TCP integration coverage, though it still stops short of fuller nREPL stream semantics like async output or interrupts.

description: Routed host-side stdout/stderr through a scoped interop capture sink and surfaced captured output in remote eval/load-file responses, with focused unit and TCP integration coverage.

code_quality.rationale: The request-scoped stdin slice fits the existing remote REPL design cleanly: it adds a small scoped input stack alongside the prior output capture, threads optional stdin through eval/load-file and describe without widening scope, and backs the behavior with focused unit plus TCP integration coverage, though it still stops short of fuller nREPL interaction semantics like async streams or interrupts.

description: Added request-scoped stdin plumbing for remote eval/load-file, threading optional JSON stdin through scoped interop input capture and focused unit plus TCP integration coverage for connection-local and logical sessions.

code_quality.rationale: Request ids and streamed stdout/stderr frames extend the remote REPL cleanly through shared interop sinks and focused unit/TCP coverage, but the protocol remains a minimal custom JSON/TCP layer rather than fuller nREPL semantics like interrupts or richer async messaging.

description: Added optional request ids plus streamed stdout/stderr frames for remote eval/load-file, echoing ids in terminal responses and covering connection-local and logical-session streaming.
Added Json.encode/1, Json.decode/1, Json.encode_pretty/1 stdlib module backed by serde_json with 14 focused unit tests.
Added Toml.encode/1 and Toml.decode/1 stdlib module backed by toml crate with 11 focused unit tests.
